vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The biome is in a state of severe degradation. The registry-defined specimens are largely absent or non-viable.

inventory_reconciliation.P1 (String of Nickels): Systemic Loss. No visible foliage; pot contains only substrate.
inventory_reconciliation.P2 (Mexican Mint): Systemic Loss. No visible foliage; pot contains only substrate.
inventory_reconciliation.P3 (Pothos): Systemic Loss. The white rabbit anchor is present, but the Pothos specimen is absent.

plant_audit.P1: Empty substrate observed. No signs of life.
plant_audit.P2: Empty substrate observed. No signs of life.
plant_audit.P3: White rabbit anchor present; soil surface is bare.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Substrate appears dry and compacted across all pots.
biome_observations.debris: Fragmented white material (possibly crushed eggshell or mineral deposits) noted in the P3/P4 pot area.

temporal_deltas.T-1_to_CURRENT: Recovery of visual data confirms the absence of all registered plant life. The biome has transitioned from a planted state to a dormant/empty state.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is currently non-functional. All registered plants have suffered total mortality or removal. The environment is currently just a collection of pots with substrate.
vision_report.anomalies: The presence of white particulate matter in the P3 pot suggests a previous intervention or soil amendment that did not result in plant survival.
vision_report.narrative_description: Upon meticulous audit, the biome registry is found to be entirely disconnected from the physical reality of the pots. The pots are devoid of the expected botanical specimens (P1-P4). The white rabbit anchor remains in P3, but the Pothos is gone. The environment is currently a sterile collection of soil-filled containers with no active biological growth.
